perturbation.original_text: LBIO shall pay all undisputed invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt of such invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: LBIO shall pay all undisputed invoices within sixty (60) days of receipt of such invoice.
perturbation.explanation: The change from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days for invoice payment creates an in-text contradiction with Section 8.2, which specifies a thirty (30)-day cure period for breaches. This inserted perturbation regarding LBIO's payment obligations introduces legal uncertainty as it is unclear whether LBIO has thirty (30) or sixty (60) days to pay invoices, potentially leading to premature breach claims if MD Anderson incorrectly assumes a breach after thirty (30) days. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the payment deadline and what terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement for the material breach or default of any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement by the other Party upon thirty (30) days' written notice and the opportunity to cure during such notice period; and such termination shall be in addition to any other remedies that it may have at law or in equity. Additionally, LBIO may terminate this Agreement if MD Anderson is declared insolvent or enters into liquidation or has a receiver or an administrator appointed over all or any part of its assets or ceases or threatens to cease to carry on business, or a resolution is passed or a petition presented to any court for the winding up of the Party or for the granting of an administration order in respect of MD Anderson, or any proceedings are commenced relating to the insolvency or possible insolvency of MD Anderson.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the invoice payment timeframe from 30 to 60 days. The contradicted text allows either party to terminate the agreement for a material breach if the other party fails to cure the breach within 30 days of written notice. Because the changed text gives LBIO 60 days to pay undisputed invoices, but the contradicted text allows MD Anderson to declare a breach if payment is not made within 30 days of written notice, there is an in-text contradiction. It is unclear when a breach occurs and when MD Anderson can terminate the agreement, thus, making the contract ambiguous and introducing the possibility of premature breach claims.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of the invalidity of any provisions of this Agreement containing any gaps, the Parties agree that such invalidity or gap shall not affect the validity of the remaining provisions of this Agreement. The Parties will replace an invalid provision or fill any gaps with valid provisions, which most closely approximate the purpose and economic effect of the invalid provision or, in the case of a gap, the Parties' presumable intentions.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of the invalidity of any provisions of this Agreement containing any gaps, the Parties agree that such invalidity or gap shall affect the validity of the remaining provisions of this Agreement. The Parties will not replace an invalid provision or fill any gaps with valid provisions, which most closely approximate the purpose and economic effect of the invalid provision or, in the case of a gap, the Parties' presumable intentions.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation in Section 9.10, directly contradicts the standard severability principle. The original clause maintains that the rest of the contract remains valid, even if a provision is invalid. The changed clause states that the invalidity of any provision affects the validity of the entire agreement. This in-text contradiction creates significant uncertainty because it is unclear whether the whole contract collapses if a single clause is deemed unenforceable or whether the agreement remains valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire understandings of the Parties and supersedes all previous agreements (oral and written), negotiations and discussions with respect to the subject matter herein. The Parties may modify any of the provisions hereof only by an instrument in writing duly executed by the Parties. No waiver of any rights under this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing signed by the Party to be charged.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9.10 directly contradicts the principle of severability, which is implied in the contradicted text. The original text ensures that the rest of the agreement remains valid even if some provisions are invalid. The changed text explicitly states that if any provision is invalid, the entire agreement is affected, thereby contradicting the purpose and implicit assumption that the agreement as a whole should be upheld as much as possible as stated in the contradicted text, which speaks to the agreement containing the 'entire understandings of the Parties.'
